title: Kaempferia philippiana
intro: Kaempferia philippiana, also known as Thai Angelica or Ching Bor, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kaempferia philippiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kaempferia philippiana is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, or consumed as a tea to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Kaempferia philippiana is a species within the Zingiberaceae family, commonly known as the ginger family. This family consists of herbaceous plants, with Kaempferia philippiana being a type of rhizomatous perennial. Kaempferia philippiana belongs to the genus Kaempferia, which consists of various species of rhizomatous perennials native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Kaempferia is further classified under the order Zingiberales, which includes several families of herbaceous plants. As a member of the Zingiberaceae family, Kaempferia philippiana shares characteristics with other ginger family species.
